title: Plan the layer stackup before routing
description: Define signal, plane, and power layers and their spacing up front so impedance and return paths are designed, not discovered.
body: Use when starting a multilayer board with any high-speed or controlled-impedance nets. Do not route first and assign layers later, because the stackup determines achievable impedance and return-path continuity. Actions: choose the layer count, place reference planes adjacent to signal layers, and verify the dielectric heights support the target impedance. Validation signals include a fab-confirmed stackup whose impedance calculations match the target and every signal layer paired with an adjacent plane. Risk boundary: a stackup change after routing forces a re-spin, so escalate stackup decisions to review before committing layout.
